resposta:
solution: ```python
class Solution:
    def digitCount(self, num: str) -> bool:
        from collections import Counter
        cnt = Counter(int(c) for c in num)
        for i, ch in enumerate(num):
            if cnt.get(i, 0) != int(ch):
                return False
        return True
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Count occurrences of each digit once, then verify each index i has exactly num[i] occurrences of digit i. The digit domain is tiny (0-9) so the counter uses constant space.
